description: PLMKR Playlist and DSP knowledge base — load order for knowledge assembly

files.description: Strategist identity and mission (placement probability x post-placement retention), jurisdiction (owns/does-not-own), six philosophy principles, decision style and biases incl. soft/hard blocks and escalation line, communication style, hard refusals (no guarantees, no pay-for-play, named fit rationale, run the gate before refusing)

Gatekeeper pitch theory (lower the risk of yes, specificity, one ask, proof hierarchy), pre-send checklist, pitch-construction bars, DSP pitch tools (fields gate / prose persuades), editorial vs independent lead-time norms, release-day discipline, anti-spam cadence rules

Marketplace and algorithm reasoning, token fit-gate then outcome ranking, the three playlist economies (editorial/algorithmic/independent) never aggregated, the editorial decision model, post-placement analytics and retention as the second KPI, no invented benchmark bands

Trust-account model and cadence logic, working cadence numbers (A/B/C follow-up, daily ceiling), DSP editorial posture (tool is the relationship), independent curator vetting and outreach, relationship-state to pitch-decision table (cold/opened/warm/damaged/closed)

Platform-as-gatekeeper model (editorial vs algorithmic access), paid-promotion decision logic (amplify never manufacture), chart-eligibility framework; per-platform editorial+algorithmic mechanics, follower-reach feed, discovery surfaces, radio/autoplay, paid tools, distributor-gated vs self-serve access, post-release windows, video-first dynamics

Territory editorial reasoning (territory != translation, distribution gates access, local programming moments, cultural chart weight); per-region ecosystems and access realities for Korea, Japan, Southeast Asia, China, India, MENA, and Afrobeats/Sub-Saharan Africa

Three-band scoring (MEASURED/CHECKED/JUDGED), Band B checkable gates (fit gate, pitch-quality bars, cadence, timing, target-health vet) with hard/soft blocks, Band C judged dimensions P1-P5 + Specificity, closed verdict scale, confidence caps, placement-probability language, deferred numeric layer with unlock

Mandatory core (work product, decision summary, confidence), conditional-module matrix, three deliverable templates (Pitch Package, Strategy Memo, Post-Placement Read), refusal-with-next-step discipline, handoff boundaries and escalation flags

notes.entity_wall: These files are PLMKR-owned originals. Do not reference external sources, rubric identifiers, sibling products, or entity names from any source material.
notes.mock_mode: Deterministic knowledge assembly only — no live LLM/API calls during loading or testing.
